content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcal PRACTrces CoMMrssroN
,128 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA 958 l.l-2329
(916\ 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
September 12,2008
Ernest Silva
Mr,rrdoch Walrath & Holmes
I130 K Street, Suite 210
Sacramento, California 958 14
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-08-147
Dear Mr. Silva:
This letter is in response to your reqlrest for advice regarding the lobbying
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Becau.se you seek g"n".ol guido,r".,
we are treating yollr request as one for informal assistance.r Additionaiy, pleaie note
that the Commission does not provide advice relating to past conduct. This letter shoulcl
not be construed as assistance on any conduct that may have already taken place. (See
RegLrlation I 8329(bX8XA).)
QUESTION
Does Section 86205(t) prohibit your t)rm, which provides both advocacy and
consulting services to school districts and charter schools, and members of your firm who
are registered lobbyists, from receiving a fee based on a percentage of the funds your
client is awarded from a state agency, where your firm's services consist of (a) assisting
with the compilation of information necessary to obtain state funding, (b) meeting with
state agency representatives to answer qnestions about your client's submittals, and (c)
meeting with state agency representatives to explain how your client's project meets the
agency' s requirements?
1 Th. Political Retirrm Act is contuined in Government Code Sections 8l(XX) throu-ch 91014. All
statuk)ry ret'erences are to the Gol'ernment Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are c()ntained in Sections llJllo throu-rrh 18997 of litle 2 of the Calitirrnia
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of the Calif'ornia Code of
Re_uulations. urrless tltherw'i sc ind icated.
- Intirrmal assistance does not provide the rr'qucstor with the immunity provicled by an opinion pr
tirrmal written advice. (secrion tt-] I l4: Regulation ltt329(cx3). copy enclosec.l.)


File No. I-08-147
Page No.2
CONCLUSION
No. Section 86205(0 does not prohibit a lobbying firm from receiving a fee
based on a percentage of the funds received by its client from a state agency where the
services provided by the firm consist of assisting the client in obtaining the state funding
and the payment is not contingent upon a proposed legislative or administrative action.
FACTS
You are a registered lobbyist with Murdoch Walrath & Holmes, a firm that
provides both advocacy and consulting services for school districts and charter schools
involved in the School Facility Program ("SFP") under Education Code Section 17070.10
et seq. The SFP provides state funding in phases for building school facilities
("preliminary apportionments," "advanced apportionments," and "final apportionments.")
Among the services you would like to provide for your clients for a fee based on a
percentage of the funds they receive are (a) assistance in gathering information for their
applications for advanced apportionments and final apportionments, (b) meeting with
state agency representatives to answer questions about the applications submitted by your
client and (c) meeting with state agency representatives to explain how your client's
project meets the agency's requirements. You have asked us four questions based on
these facts. However, based on our analysis of your first question, we need not address
your other questions.
ANALYSIS
Section 86205(0 prohibits a lobbyist or lobbying firm from accepting or
agreeing to accept any payment in any way contingent upon the defeat, enactment, or
outcome of any proposed legislative or administrative action." Clearly, your facts do not
implicate any legislative action. (See Section82037.) We, therefore, turn our analysis to
the term "administrative action" which is defined in Section 82002(a) as follows:
"(a) 'Administrative action' means the proposal, drafting,
development, consideration, amendment, enactment, or
defeat by any state agency of any rule, regulation, or other
action in any ratemaking proceeding or any quasi-
legislative proceeding, which shall include any proceeding
governed by Chapter 3.5 (commencing with Section 11340)
of Part I of Division 3 of Title 2."
A determination by the state agency whether to fund your client's project does not
involve a "proposal, drafting, development, consideration, enactment, or defeat by any
state agency of any mle, or regulation or other action in any ratemaking proceeding."
Further, such a determination does not involve a "proposal" or "consideration" of "other
action" in a "quasi-legislative proceeding" inasmuch as Regulation 18202(a)(6) provides


File No. I-08-147
Page No. 3
that a proceeding in which action is taken "awarding a grant or contract" is not a "qlrasi-
legislative proceeding" for the pLuposes of Section 82002. Hence, the provisions of the
Act, and specifically the prohibition of Section 86205(0, are not applicable to
proceedings regarding the awarding of a grant, such as an SFP grant, by a state agency.
In light of the analysis above, we conclude that the prohibition in Section
86205(0 does not prevent your lobbying firm from receiving a fee for assisting school
districts and charter schools in pursuit of SFP funds in the form of a percentage of the
ftrnds awarded to your client by a state agency because the payment is not made
contingent upon a proposed legislative or administrative action.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division
vJjgl
Enclosure
